title: Day 13 Interview Prep
description: Practice React Router, dynamic routing, URL params, and navigation patterns.

questions.question: How do dynamic routes work in React Router?
questions.sampleAnswer: I define path 'users/:userId' in route config. React Router matches /users/1 and /users/42. UserProfilePage reads userId via useParams and fetches that user. One component handles all user IDs — data comes from URL.

questions.question: What is the difference between Link and useNavigate?
questions.sampleAnswer: Link is declarative — I use it in user lists and nav menus. useNavigate returns a function for programmatic navigation — after delete confirm, redirect on 404, or navigate(-1) to go back. Both use client-side routing without reload.

questions.question: What is optional chaining and when do you use it on profile pages?
questions.sampleAnswer: Optional chaining (?.) stops property access when intermediate value is null or undefined. On profiles, user?.company?.name prevents crash when company is missing. I combine with ?? for display fallbacks like 'N/A'.

questions.question: How do you type useParams in TypeScript?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Route param typing in production apps.
questions.sampleAnswer: I define type UserRouteParams = { userId: string }. useParams<UserRouteParams>() gives typed userId as string | undefined. I guard if (!userId) and parse Number(userId) with validation. User data interface is separate — params type only describes URL.

questions.question: User navigates /users/1 then /users/2. Profile shows stale user 1 data. Fix?
keyPoints: Reset loading state on param change
questions.sampleAnswer: useEffect likely missing userId dependency — fetches only on mount. Fix: useEffect(() => { fetchUser(userId) }, [userId]). Clear user state or show loading when userId changes. Add cleanup flag from Day 08 for race between slow requests.

questions.question: How do you implement a Not Found page for invalid routes and invalid user IDs?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: 404 handling at route and data level.
keyPoints: Different copy for route vs resource 404
questions.sampleAnswer: Route level: { path: '*', element: <NotFoundPage /> } catches bad URLs. Resource level: fetch user by ID, if 404 response render NotFound or inline message. I validate userId is numeric before fetch. User sees 'User not found' vs 'Page not found'.

questions.question: Compare URL params, query strings, and route state for passing data.
keyPoints: State is ephemeral — lost on refresh
Params enable deep linking and sharing
questions.sampleAnswer: URL params identify resources — /users/42 is shareable and bookmarkable. Query strings modify view — /products?category=electronics. navigate state passes data without URL but lost on refresh — OK for transient UI, not primary data. Profile ID belongs in params.

questions.question: Cart Context stops working after adding React Router. Diagnose.
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Provider tree and SPA navigation integration.
questions.sampleAnswer: Likely CartProvider is inside one route instead of wrapping Router — navigating unmounts provider. Or team used <a href> causing full reload resetting state. Fix: CartProvider at root above BrowserRouter. All navigation via Link/useNavigate.
redFlags: Suggest full page reload for navigation
